records.expectedContractOutcome: Candidate source state builds the zevm binary and default Zig test graph without ad-hoc dependency or path edits.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/release_metadata.zig:release tuple generator emits valid json
records.expectedContractOutcome: release-tuple.json has the required URL-bound identifier, schema version, exact field set, pin tuple values, and commit identifier equality.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/release_metadata.zig:light default checkpoints generator emits valid json
records.expectedContractOutcome: light-default-checkpoints.json has the required URL-bound identifier, schema version, exact field set, and 32-byte checkpoint defaults for mainnet, sepolia, and holesky.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/release_metadata.zig:asset name validator catches missing and duplicate required assets
records.expectedContractOutcome: A metadata-backed release identifier is valid only when release-tuple.json and light-default-checkpoints.json are both present exactly once.
Every qualification row carries the required surface, section, category, assertion type, assertion identifier, expected outcome, and explicit coverage status fields.
CLI/config loading applies shared defaults, single-mode validation, mode-specific flag rejection, precedence, structured mining/fork validation, and light checkpoint input precedence.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/config_test.zig; src/node/runtime_test.zig:NodeRuntime.init rejects invalid startup config before listener setup; src/main.zig
records.expectedContractOutcome: Invalid startup inputs fail during configuration resolution before the HTTP listener is treated as ready.
Trusted mode owns writable local state, deterministic genesis/runtime defaults, snapshots/reset behavior, fork overlays, mining modes, and block environment controls.
Light mode resolves supported networks/checkpoints, applies consensus bootstrap/update checks, and reports sync status lifecycle fields.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/rpc/server_test.zig:non-root request target returns 404 without JSON-RPC body; non-POST request returns 405; missing or invalid content-type returns 415 without JSON-RPC body; single notification returns 204 empty response; notification-only batch returns 204 empty response
records.expectedContractOutcome: HTTP JSON-RPC accepts only POST / with application/json, returns contract HTTP status codes, and handles notification-only requests with 204 empty bodies.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/rpc/listener_smoke_test.zig:trusted mode serves JSON-RPC over a real TCP listener; trusted mode returns 204 for a notification over a real TCP listener
records.expectedContractOutcome: A release verification test binds the real HTTP listener socket in trusted mode and completes a JSON-RPC request over TCP.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/rpc/listener_smoke_test.zig:light mode restarts from persisted checkpoint over a real TCP listener
records.expectedContractOutcome: A release verification test binds the real HTTP listener socket in light mode and validates startup plus restart/resume from a persisted checkpoint input path.
JSON-RPC 2.0 request parsing, batch handling, ID preservation, method-not-found, invalid params, and error envelope semantics match the contract.

Trusted core read methods return chain ID, block number, account balance/code/storage/nonce, accounts, coinbase, gas price, blob base fee, and fee history according to phase-1 selectors and tuple rules.
Trusted simulation and submission support eth_call, eth_estimateGas, eth_sendTransaction, and eth_sendRawTransaction while rejecting unsupported typed or dynamic transaction forms.
Trusted canonical block, transaction, receipt, block receipt, and log query methods follow nullability, selector, index, ordering, and payload field contracts.

records.assertionIdentifier: src/rpc/dispatch_wiring_test.zig; src/rpc/trusted_fork_handlers.zig; src/rpc/handlers/dev_erc20_test.zig; src/rpc/handlers/txpool_test.zig; src/rpc/handlers/simulation_test.zig; src/rpc/handlers/block_query_handlers_test.zig; src/node/runtime_test.zig
records.expectedContractOutcome: Trusted dev-node controls mutate local accounts, storage, code, snapshots, impersonation, txpool, mining, time, coinbase, block gas, fee, and fork metadata inside the documented phase-1 boundaries.

Light mode exposes zevm_lightSyncStatus, eth_chainId, eth_blockNumber, and proof-backed balance/code/storage/nonce reads with readiness, retained-window, proof-verification, and mode-unsupported errors.
Out-of-contract methods return method-not-found, and phase-1 light-mode unsupported trusted families return the documented mode-unsupported error.
Official Ethereum state, blockchain, and execution-spec fixture coverage either passes in the release gate or has explicit phase-1 exclusions.
Hive rpc-compat lifecycle coverage either passes in the release gate or has explicit phase-1 exclusions.
